lesson_title: Travel, Culture and Narratives: Reading Lesson 3
learning_objective: Learners can explain, narrate, give reasons and manage familiar situations in a reading task about relationships.
cefr_outcome: Learners can deal with most situations while travelling and produce simple connected text on familiar topics.
learner_facing_explanation: In this lesson you will practise reading for travel, culture and narratives. You will learn useful language connected to relationships and practise relative clauses. By the end, you should be able to complete a short real-life task and explain your answer clearly at B1 level.
complete_teaching_input: Focus language: relative clauses. Vocabulary theme: relationships. The teacher introduces the target language in a meaningful context, models pronunciation and form, checks meaning with concept questions, and moves learners from controlled practice to freer use. At B1, learners should explain, narrate, give reasons and manage familiar situations with increasing independence. The lesson should use board examples, short teacher modelling, pair practice, feedback and a final communicative task.
examples: Example 1: I can use relative clauses to talk about relationships.
Example 2: The learner gives a B1 answer with clear meaning and correct key language.
Example 3: The learner checks, improves and repeats the answer after feedback.
Example 4: The learner uses one linking phrase or support phrase suitable for B1.

interactive_practice.prompt: Choose the best sentence using relative clauses.
options: Correct use of relative clauses in a B1 sentence.
Incorrect word order with relative clauses.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Correct. The sentence uses relative clauses accurately.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Check the form and meaning of relative clauses.

interactive_practice.prompt: Match the relationships phrase to its meaning.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Good matching. You understand the phrase meaning.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Look again at the context and key words.

interactive_practice.prompt: Build a complete answer for the lesson task.
interactive_practice.model_answer: I can explain my idea clearly because I understand relationships.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Well done. Your sentence is complete and meaningful.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Check that your sentence has a subject, verb and clear meaning.

correct_answers.P3: I can explain my idea clearly because I understand relationships.

instant_feedback.correct: Correct. Continue to the next step.
instant_feedback.partly_correct: Good start. Check accuracy and add one more detail.
instant_feedback.incorrect: Try again. Review the model and look at the key language.

teacher_notes: Begin with a quick diagnostic question. Model the target form relative clauses. Use choral repetition if pronunciation is important. Monitor pair work and record three common errors. Finish with delayed correction and one confidence-building success point.
homework_task: Write or record five sentences about relationships using language from this lesson.

assessment_task.task_title: B1 Reading Check - Travel, Culture and Narratives
assessment_task.instructions: Complete a short reading task using relative clauses and vocabulary from relationships.
success_criteria: Uses relative clauses with reasonable accuracy
Responds at the expected CEFR level

criteria.name: Language control
criteria.descriptor: Uses relative clauses and sentence structure accurately.

criteria.name: Vocabulary range
criteria.descriptor: Uses appropriate words connected to relationships.

criteria.name: Fluency/coherence
criteria.descriptor: Organises ideas clearly for the level.

speaking_task: Speak for 60-120 seconds about relationships. Use at least two examples and one reason.
writing_task: Write a short B1 response about relationships. Include the target language: relative clauses.

listening_audio.script: Audio script for B1-M03-L03. Speaker one: Today we are practising relationships. Speaker two: I need to use relative clauses correctly. Speaker one: Listen carefully and choose the answer that best matches the meaning.

visual_activity_cards.title: Relative Clauses form
visual_activity_cards.alt_text: Visual grammar card for relative clauses
